Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum of pairwise products: a[0]*a[1] + a[1]*a[2] + ...

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module PairProdSum
  (defun pps (a n)
    (def s 0)
    (def i 1)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (set s (+ s (* (arr.get a i) (arr.get a (- i 1)))))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    s)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3) (arr.set a 3 4)
    (assert-eq (pps a 4) 20))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write a short status string and return the write-call's status code.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SaveStatus
  (extern defun file_write ((path : Str) (body : Str)) : Num @capability "file.write")
  (defun save_status (path n)
    (file_write path (str.from_num n)))
  (test ok (mocks (file.write "/tmp/s.txt" 1)) (assert-eq (save_status "/tmp/s.txt" 7) 1))
  (test zero (mocks (file.write "/tmp/s.txt" 1)) (assert-eq (save_status "/tmp/s.txt" 0) 1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper water: tier1_use, tier2_use, tier3_use, tax, total + round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Water5
  (defun min2 (a b)
    (if (< a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun max2 (a b)
    (if (> a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun tier1 (gallons)
    (* (min2 gallons 1000) 0.005))
  (defun tier2 (gallons)
    (* (max2 0 (- (min2 gallons 5000) 1000)) 0.010))
  (defun tier3 (gallons)
    (* (max2 0 (- gallons 5000)) 0.020))
  (defun apply_tax (amt pct)
    (* amt (+ 1 (/ pct 100))))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun bill (gallons tax_pct)
    (def sub (+ (tier1 gallons) (+ (tier2 gallons) (tier3 gallons))))
    (round2 (apply_tax sub tax_pct)))
  (test small (near? (bill 500 0) 2.5 0.01))
  (test mid (near? (bill 3000 0) 25.0 0.01))
  (test high (near? (bill 7000 0) 85.0 0.01))
  (test taxed (near? (bill 3000 8) 27.0 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Consultant gross = hours×rate; if hours=0 return 0; FICA=7.65%; net = gross-FICA. Round to 2dp.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Consult
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))
  (defun net_consult (hours rate)
    (if (= hours 0.0) (return 0.0))
    (def gross (* hours rate))
    (def fica (* gross 0.0765))
    (round2 (- gross fica)))
  (test zero (near? (net_consult 0.0 100.0) 0.0 0.01))
  (test ten_hours (near? (net_consult 10.0 100.0) 923.5 0.01))
  (test small (near? (net_consult 1.0 50.0) 46.18 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Convert binary number (passed as decimal) to decimal value.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Bin2Dec
  (defun bin_to_dec (bits)
    (def x bits)
    (def total 0)
    (def power 1)
    (while (> x 0)
      (def d (math_mod x 10))
      (if (= d 1) (set total (+ total power)))
      (set power (* power 2))
      (set x (math_floor (/ x 10))))
    total)
  (test zero (eq? (bin_to_dec 0) 0))
  (test one (eq? (bin_to_dec 1) 1))
  (test ten (eq? (bin_to_dec 10) 2))
  (test eleven (eq? (bin_to_dec 11) 3))
  (test full (eq? (bin_to_dec 1010) 10))
  (test eight (eq? (bin_to_dec 1000) 8)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

  (test n0 (eq? (tri 0) 0))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Count how many values fall in [lo, hi).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HistBucket
  (defun count_range (a n lo hi)
    (def c 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def v (arr.get a i))
        (if (and (>= v lo) (< v hi)) (set c (+ c 1)) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    c)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 6))
    (arr.set a 0 5) (arr.set a 1 12) (arr.set a 2 8) (arr.set a 3 15) (arr.set a 4 3) (arr.set a 5 9)
    (assert-eq (count_range a 6 0 10) 4)
    (assert-eq (count_range a 6 10 20) 2))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper truck: monthly_lease, mileage_charge, fuel_cost, maintenance, total + round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Truck5
  (defun max2 (a b)
    (if (> a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun monthly_lease (rate)
    rate)
  (defun mileage_charge (miles included rate)
    (* (max2 0 (- miles included)) rate))
  (defun fuel_cost (miles mpg gas_price)
    (* (/ miles mpg) gas_price))
  (defun maintenance (miles rate_per_mile)
    (* miles rate_per_mile))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun monthly_total (lease miles included mile_rate mpg gas maint_rate)
    (round2 (+ (monthly_lease lease)
               (+ (mileage_charge miles included mile_rate)
                  (+ (fuel_cost miles mpg gas)
                     (maintenance miles maint_rate))))))
  (test under (near? (monthly_total 500 800 1000 0.20 15 4 0.05) 753.33 0.1))
  (test over (near? (monthly_total 500 1500 1000 0.20 15 4 0.05) 1075.0 0.1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return smallest divisor > 1 (so for primes it's n itself).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SmallestDiv
  (defun sd (n)
    (def d 2)
    (def found 0)
    (while (and (= found 0) (<= (* d d) n))
      (do
        (if (= (math.mod n d) 0) (set found 1) (set d (+ d 1)))))
    (if (= found 0) n d))
  (test prime (eq? (sd 13) 13))
  (test even (eq? (sd 12) 2))
  (test n9 (eq? (sd 9) 3))
  (test n2 (eq? (sd 2) 2)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Return 1 if any pair of distinct indices sums to target, 0 otherwise.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module TwoSum
  (defun has2 (a n target)
    (def found 0)
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def j (+ i 1))
        (while (< j n)
          (do
            (if (= (+ (arr.get a i) (arr.get a j)) target) (set found 1) 0)
            (set j (+ j 1))))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    found)
  (test yes (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 2) (arr.set a 1 7) (arr.set a 2 11) (arr.set a 3 15)
    (assert-eq (has2 a 4 9) 1)))
  (test no (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 3))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3)
    (assert-eq (has2 a 3 100) 0))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

5-helper card: balance_after_payment, interest_charge, late_fee, min_due, total + round2.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Card5
  (defun max2 (a b)
    (if (> a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun balance_after (current payment)
    (max2 0 (- current payment)))
  (defun interest_charge (balance apr)
    (/ (* balance (/ apr 100)) 12))
  (defun late_fee (paid_late)
    (if (= paid_late 1) (return 35))
    0)
  (defun min_due (balance)
    (def calc (* balance 0.02))
    (if (< calc 25) (return 25))
    calc)
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun statement (current payment apr late)
    (def b (balance_after current payment))
    (def i (interest_charge b apr))
    (def f (late_fee late))
    (round2 (+ b (+ i f))))
  (test paid_in_full (near? (statement 1000 1000 18 0) 0.0 0.01))
  (test partial (near? (statement 1000 500 18 0) 507.5 0.05))
  (test late (near? (statement 1000 500 18 1) 542.5 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Cabinets + counters + appliances + labor + permit.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Kitchen
  (defun cabinet_cost (linear_ft per_ft)
    (* linear_ft per_ft))
  (defun counter_cost (sq_ft per_sq)
    (* sq_ft per_sq))
  (defun appliances (count avg)
    (* count avg))
  (defun labor (hours rate)
    (* hours rate))
  (defun permit (sub pct)
    (* sub (/ pct 100)))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun total (cab_ft cab_rate counter_sq counter_rate appl_count appl_avg hours hourly permit_pct)
    (def sub (+ (cabinet_cost cab_ft cab_rate)
                (+ (counter_cost counter_sq counter_rate)
                   (+ (appliances appl_count appl_avg)
                      (labor hours hourly)))))
    (round2 (+ sub (permit sub permit_pct))))
  (test small (near? (total 10 200 20 50 3 500 30 75 0) 6750.0 0.5))
  (test full (near? (total 20 300 40 100 5 800 60 100 5) 21000.0 1.0)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Reverse a string by iterating with str.substring.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module StrReverse
  (defun rev (s)
    (def n (str.length s))
    (def out "")
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (set out (str.concat (str.substring s i 1) out))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    out)
  (test hello (eq? (rev "hello") "olleh"))
  (test empty (eq? (rev "") ""))
  (test single (eq? (rev "x") "x"))
  (test pal (eq? (rev "abba") "abba")))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Daily ml = weight_kg * 35, bumped by activity_level (0=1.0,1=1.2,2=1.5).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Water
  (defun activity_factor (level)
    (if (= level 0) (return 1.0))
    (if (= level 1) (return 1.2))
    1.5)
  (defun daily_ml (kg level)
    (* (* kg 35) (activity_factor level)))
  (test sedentary (near? (daily_ml 70 0) 2450.0 0.01))
  (test light (near? (daily_ml 70 1) 2940.0 0.01))
  (test active (near? (daily_ml 70 2) 3675.0 0.01)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Count digits in string.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module CountDigits
  (defun is_digit (c)
    (if (str_eq c "0") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "1") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "2") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "3") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "4") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "5") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "6") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "7") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "8") (return 1))
    (if (str_eq c "9") (return 1))
    0)
  (defun count_digits (s)
    (def n (str_length s))
    (def i 0)
    (def total 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (set total (+ total (is_digit (str_substring s i 1))))
      (set i (+ i 1)))
    total)
  (test all_letters (eq? (count_digits "hello") 0))
  (test all_digits (eq? (count_digits "12345") 5))
  (test mixed (eq? (count_digits "abc123def") 3)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Solar: monthly_savings * 12 = annual.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module SolarS
  (defun min2 (a b)
    (if (< a b) (return a))
    b)
  (defun monthly_generated (panels watt sun days)
    (/ (* panels (* watt (* sun days))) 1000))
  (defun monthly_savings (used generated rate)
    (* (min2 used generated) rate))
  (defun annual_savings (panels watt sun days used rate)
    (def gen (monthly_generated panels watt sun days))
    (* (monthly_savings used gen rate) 12))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun yearly (panels watt sun days used rate)
    (round2 (annual_savings panels watt sun days used rate)))
  (test small (near? (yearly 5 250 5 30 800 0.15) 337.5 0.05))
  (test full (near? (yearly 10 300 6 30 540 0.20) 1296.0 0.05)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

  (test exact (mocks (env.get "E" "hello")) (eq? (matches "E" "hello") 1))
  (test cased (mocks (env.get "E" "HELLO")) (eq? (matches "E" "hello") 1))
  (test diff (mocks (env.get "E" "world")) (eq? (matches "E" "hello") 0)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Session: per-hour + group discount + materials.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Tutor
  (defun base_rate (hours rate)
    (* hours rate))
  (defun group_discount (cost students)
    (if (>= students 4) (return (* cost 0.7)))
    (if (>= students 2) (return (* cost 0.85)))
    cost)
  (defun materials_fee (count)
    (* count 5))
  (defun round2 (x)
    (/ (math_floor (+ (* x 100) 0.5)) 100))
  (defun total (hours rate students materials)
    (round2 (+ (group_discount (base_rate hours rate) students) (materials_fee materials))))
  (test solo (near? (total 1 50 1 0) 50.0 0.01))
  (test pair (near? (total 1 50 2 0) 42.5 0.01))
  (test group (near? (total 1 50 4 0) 35.0 0.01))
  (test with_mat (near? (total 1 50 1 2) 60.0 0.01)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Read file, return 'EMPTY', 'SHORT' (<10 bytes), or 'LONG'.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module FileSizeClass
  (extern defun file_read (p) @capability "file.read")
  (defun classify (p)
    (def s (file_read p))
    (def n (str_length s))
    (if (= n 0) (return "EMPTY"))
    (if (< n 10) (return "SHORT"))
    "LONG")
  (test empty (mocks (file.read "/f" "")) (eq? (classify "/f") "EMPTY"))
  (test short (mocks (file.read "/f" "hi")) (eq? (classify "/f") "SHORT"))
  (test long (mocks (file.read "/f" "hello world data")) (eq? (classify "/f") "LONG")))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Subtract mean from each, divide by stddev; check first element approx.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module Zscore
  (defun mean (a n)
    (def s 0.0) (def i 0)
    (while (< i n) (do (set s (+ s (arr.get a i))) (set i (+ i 1))))
    (/ s n))
  (defun stddev (a n mu)
    (def s 0.0) (def i 0)
    (while (< i n)
      (do
        (def d (- (arr.get a i) mu))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (math.sqrt (/ s n)))
  (defun z_first (a n)
    (def mu (mean a n))
    (def sd (stddev a n mu))
    (/ (- (arr.get a 0) mu) sd))
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 2.0) (arr.set a 1 4.0) (arr.set a 2 4.0) (arr.set a 3 4.0) (arr.set a 4 6.0)
    (assert-near (z_first a 5) -1.5811388 0.0001))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

  (test zero (near? (ssqrt 0.0) 0.0 0.0001))
  (test four (near? (ssqrt 4.0) 2.0 0.0001))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Map x -> sign(x) (-1/0/1), sum the results.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HofSign
  (defun sgn (x) (if (> x 0) 1 (if (< x 0) -1 0)))
  (defun add2 (a b) (+ a b))
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 -3) (arr.set a 1 0) (arr.set a 2 5) (arr.set a 3 -1) (arr.set a 4 2)
    (def s : (Array Num) (arr.map a sgn))
    (assert-eq (arr.reduce s add2 0) 0))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Compute (base^exp) mod m using repeated squaring.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ModExp
  (defun modexp (base exp m)
    (def r 1)
    (def b (math.mod base m))
    (def e exp)
    (while (> e 0)
      (do
        (if (= (math.mod e 2) 1) (set r (math.mod (* r b) m)) 0)
        (set b (math.mod (* b b) m))
        (set e (/ (- e (math.mod e 2)) 2))))
    r)
  (test small (eq? (modexp 2 10 1000) 24))
  (test fermat (eq? (modexp 3 5 7) 5))
  (test one (eq? (modexp 7 0 11) 1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

(module EnvCube
  (extern defun env_read (k) @capability "env.get")
  (defun cube (k)
    (def v (env_read k))
    (if (str_eq v "") (return 0))
    (def n (str_to_num v))
    (* n (* n n)))
  (test empty (mocks (env.get "X" "")) (eq? (cube "X") 0))
  (test two (mocks (env.get "X" "2")) (eq? (cube "X") 8))
  (test five (mocks (env.get "X" "5")) (eq? (cube "X") 125)))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Degrees to radians: r = d * pi / 180.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Trial-division primality up to sqrt(n).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module IsPrime
  (defun is_prime (n)
    (if (< n 2) (return 0))
    (if (= n 2) (return 1))
    (if (= (math_mod n 2) 0) (return 0))
    (def i 3)
    (def b (math_floor (math_sqrt n)))
    (while (<= i b)
      (if (= (math_mod n i) 0) (return 0))
      (set i (+ i 2)))
    1)
  (test two (eq? (is_prime 2) 1))
  (test three (eq? (is_prime 3) 1))
  (test four (eq? (is_prime 4) 0))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Sum of squares of the decimal digits of n (one Happy-number step).

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module HappyStep
  (defun step (n)
    (def s 0)
    (def x n)
    (while (> x 0)
      (do
        (def d (math.mod x 10))
        (set s (+ s (* d d)))
        (set x (/ (- x d) 10))))
    s)
  (test n19 (eq? (step 19) 82))
  (test n7 (eq? (step 7) 49))
  (test n1 (eq? (step 1) 1)))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Check whether an integer array reads the same forwards and backwards.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module ArrPalindrome
  (defun ispal (a n)
    (def ok 1)
    (def i 0)
    (def j (- n 1))
    (while (< i j)
      (do
        (if (not (= (arr.get a i) (arr.get a j))) (set ok 0) 0)
        (set i (+ i 1))
        (set j (- j 1))))
    ok)
  (test yes (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 5))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3) (arr.set a 3 2) (arr.set a 4 1)
    (assert-eq (ispal a 5) 1)))
  (test no (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3) (arr.set a 3 4)
    (assert-eq (ispal a 4) 0))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Period pricing + per-day insurance, then loyalty 15% off if loyalty=1, then tax_pct% on top. Round to 2 decimals.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module RentalLT
  (defun round2 (x) (/ (math.floor (+ (* x 100.0) 0.5)) 100.0))
  (defun period_base (days daily)
    (def weeks (math.floor (/ days 7.0)))
    (def rem (- days (* weeks 7.0)))
    (+ (* weeks (* 6.0 daily)) (* rem daily)))
  (defun rental_lt (days daily insurance loyalty tax_pct)
    (def base (period_base days daily))
    (def with_ins (+ base (* days insurance)))
    (def discounted (if (= loyalty 1) (* with_ins 0.85) with_ins))
    (def taxed (* discounted (+ 1.0 (/ tax_pct 100.0))))
    (round2 taxed))
  (test plain (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 370.0 0.01))
  (test eight_days (near? (rental_lt 8.0 50.0 10.0 0 0.0) 430.0 0.01))
  (test loyalty_off (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 1 0.0) 314.5 0.01))
  (test taxed (near? (rental_lt 7.0 50.0 10.0 0 10.0) 407.0 0.01)))

(module FicaOnly

    (def reg (* (math.min hours 40.0) rate))

    (+ reg ot))

    (round2 (- g (* g 0.0765))))

Write an AGC module that satisfies this objective:

Rotate array left by one position; check element at index 0.

Include self-verifying (test ...) blocks. Output ONLY the module source.

(module RotateLeft
  (defun rotL (a n)
    (def first (arr.get a 0))
    (def i 0)
    (while (< i (- n 1))
      (do
        (arr.set a i (arr.get a (+ i 1)))
        (set i (+ i 1))))
    (arr.set a (- n 1) first)
    a)
  (test t (do
    (def a : (Array Num) (arr.new 4))
    (arr.set a 0 1) (arr.set a 1 2) (arr.set a 2 3) (arr.set a 3 4)
    (rotL a 4)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 0) 2)
    (assert-eq (arr.get a 3) 1))))
Output only AGC (Agentic Compiler) S-expression source code, no prose.

(module CompoundQ

(module ValidateRoute
  (defun valid (n) (if (and (>= n 1) (<= n 5)) 1 0))
  (defun label (n)
    (if (= n 1) "one"
      (if (= n 2) "two"
        (if (= n 3) "three"
          (if (= n 4) "four"
            (if (= n 5) "five" "out"))))))
  (defun classify (n) (if (= (valid n) 1) (label n) "out"))
  (test ok (eq? (classify 3) "three"))
  (test high (eq? (classify 9) "out"))
  (test low (eq? (classify 0) "out")))

(module FB

    (if (= (math_mod n 15) 0) (return "FizzBuzz"))
    (if (= (math_mod n 3) 0) (return "Fizz"))
    (if (= (math_mod n 5) 0) (return "Buzz"))
    (str_from_num n))
